headline: Fortnite is getting a bunch of AI-powered ‘personas’
summary: Fortnite introduces 36 new AI-powered characters with unique voices and personalities. These AI NPCs will be available for creators to use in their experiences starting July 30th. Epic Games aims to enhance Fortnite experiences with more dynamic and interactive characters.

headline: Samsung’s 55-inch Frame art TV is $200 cheaper than usual
summary: Samsung's 55-inch Frame 4K TV is currently on sale, offering a $200 discount. This unique TV transforms into a piece of art when not in use. It features art-like bezels and a matte finish for a gallery aesthetic.

headline: Netflix says around 300 titles used generative AI
summary: Netflix revealed approximately 300 titles on its platform utilized generative AI. Most AI usage occurred during post-production processes. The company aims to leverage AI for higher quality, faster output, and reduced costs.

headline: Why are people buying so many CDs?
summary: CD sales saw a 16% increase in the first half of 2026 in the US, reaching 16.3 million units. Fans are reportedly buying CDs as an affordable way to support their favorite artists. This trend highlights a resurgence in physical media purchases.

headline: 'Wrong (again)' — Infinity Ward Fires Back at Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Gunny AI Chatbot Claim
summary: Infinity Ward denied claims that Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4's new Gunsmith features an AI chatbot. The developer explicitly refuted the rumor with a strong statement. This comes amid ongoing discussions about AI integration in gaming.

headline: MTG Star Trek Commander Deck Bundle Is Much Cheaper Than Buying Each Precon Deck Individually
summary: Preorders for the Magic: The Gathering Star Trek Commander decks are now live. A special bundle offers significant savings, $75 cheaper than buying decks individually. This release is noted as a better value compared to previous Marvel Super Hero Decks.

headline: Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse Might Make the Long Wait for a New 2D Castlevania Worth It | IGN Preview
summary: IGN's preview of Castlevania: Belmont's Curse suggests it lives up to high expectations. Developed by Evil Empire (Dead Cells), the game shows strong potential after three hours of play. Fans awaiting a new 2D Castlevania entry may find their patience rewarded.

headline: Cape Fear Episode 8 Recap and Review – ‘Los tiempos de Dios son Perfectos’
summary: Cape Fear Episode 8 features a grim discovery and shocking revelations about Max and Anna's past. The episode showcases Amy Adams's standout performance of the series. The recap delves into the dramatic plot points of "Los tiempos de Dios son Perfectos."

headline: Burnham's 'Manchesterism' got him to No 10 - but will it work for the UK?
summary: Economics editor Faisal Islam analyzes Andy Burnham's "Manchesterism" approach. The article questions if Manchester's regional success can be replicated nationally. It explores the potential for this model to impact UK policy.

headline: China hits out at British Steel nationalisation
summary: China has criticized the UK government's decision to nationalize British Steel. The UK asserts the move safeguards "a vital national capability." The nationalization sparks international reactions regarding industrial policy.

headline: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit
summary: The US has launched new strikes in the region, escalating tensions. Iran claims these strikes targeted and hit civilian infrastructure, including bridges. Concurrently, the US boarded a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: The financial winners and losers from the World Cup
summary: The 2026 World Cup is generating significant financial activity off the field. The article identifies which entities are profiting most and who is losing out. It explores the economic impacts and distribution of wealth surrounding the tournament.
